        spec.initContainers[*].securityContext.privileged, and
        spec.ephemeralContainers[*].securityContext.privileged must be unset or set to `false`.'
raw_log:
Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

**Structural changes**
| Old | New |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `spec.background: true` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Pod]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` with `apiGroups: ['']`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]`, `resources: [pods]` (lowercase) |

**Annotations** — dropped `kyverno.io/kyverno-version`, `kyverno.io/kubernetes-version`, and `policies.kyverno.io/subject` (Kyverno-engine-specific, not part of the new API); remaining annotations sorted alphabetically.

**Pattern → CEL expression** — the old `=(field)` optional-match syntax translates to `!has(field) ||` guards in CEL:
- `=(ephemeralContainers)` / `=(initContainers)` → `!has(object.spec.X) || ...`
- `=(securityContext)` → `!has(c.securityContext) || ...`
- `=(privileged): "false"` → `!has(c.securityContext.privileged) || c.securityContext.privileged == false`

**Message** — converted from a static `message:` string to a dynamic `messageExpression:` that prefixes the pod name for actionable error output.

description: resource requests often need to be tailored to the type of workload in the container/Pod
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_enforce_resources_as_ratio.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy resource requests often need to be tailored to the type of workload in the container/Pod. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_enforce_resources_as_ratio.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
input_snippet:
apiVersion : kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: enforce-resources-as-ratio
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Enforce Resources as Ratio
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Resource requests often need to be tailored to the type of workload in the container/Pod.
      With many different types of applications in a cluster, enforcing hard limits on requests
      or limits may not work and a ratio may be better suited instead. This policy checks every
      container in a Pod and ensures that memory limits are no more than 2.5x its requests.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  rules:
  - name: check-memory-requests-limits
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    preconditions:
      any:
      - key: "{{ request.operation || 'BACKGROUND' }}"
        operator: AnyIn
        value:
        - CREATE
        - UPDATE
    validate:
      message: Limits may not exceed 2.5x the requests.
      foreach:
      - list: "request.object.spec.containers"
        deny:
          conditions:
            any:
              # Set resources.limits.memory equal to zero if not present and resources.requests.memory equal to 1m rather than zero
              # to avoid undefined division error. No memory request in this case is basically the same as 1m. Kubernetes API server
              # will automatically set requests=limits if only limits is defined.
            - key: "{{ divide('{{ element.resources.limits.memory || '0' }}', '{{ element.resources.requests.memory || '1m' }}') }}"
              operator: GreaterThan
              value: 2.5
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Resource requests often need to be tailored to the type of workload in the
      container/Pod. With many different types of applications in a cluster,
      enforcing hard limits on requests or limits may not work and a ratio may be
      better suited instead. This policy checks every container in a Pod and
      ensures that memory limits are no more than 2.5x its requests.
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Enforce Resources as Ratio
  name: enforce-resources-as-ratio
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ''
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(c,
          !has(c.resources) ||
          !has(c.resources.limits) ||
          !('memory' in c.resources.limits) ||
          c.resources.limits['memory'].asInteger() == 0 ||
          (
            has(c.resources.requests) &&
            'memory' in c.resources.requests &&
            c.resources.requests['memory'].asInteger() > 0 &&
            c.resources.limits['memory'].asInteger() * 2 <=
              c.resources.requests['memory'].asInteger() * 5
          )
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        'Pod ' + object.metadata.name + ': memory limits may not exceed 2.5x the
        requests. Check container memory resources.'
raw_log:
Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

**Structural changes**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` → `policies.kyverno.io/v1`
- `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `ValidatingPolicy`

**Metadata**
- Dropped `kyverno.io/kyverno-version`, `policies.kyverno.io/minversion`, `kyverno.io/kubernetes-version`, and `policies.kyverno.io/subject` — the reference `after.yaml` omits these version-pinning annotations.
- Remaining annotations sorted alphabetically, `name` moved after `annotations` block (matches reference style).

**Spec rewrites**
| Old | New |
|---|---|
| `validationFailureAction: Audit` | `validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `match.any[].resources.kinds: [Pod]` | `matchConstraints.resourceRules` with `apiGroups: ['']`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, `resources: [pods]` |
| `preconditions` CREATE/UPDATE check | `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` inside the resource rule |
| `background` (implicit via BACKGROUND precondition) | `evaluation.background.enabled: true` |

**Validation logic (JMESPath `foreach`+`deny` → CEL)**

The original denies when `divide(limits.memory ‖ '0', requests.memory ‖ '1m') > 2.5`. The CEL equivalent uses `all()` to assert the inverse (allow condition) over every container:

```cel
object.spec.containers.all(c,
  !has(c.resources) ||
  !has(c.resources.limits) ||
  !('memory' in c.resources.limits) ||
  c.resources.limits['memory'].asInteger() == 0 ||
  (
    has(c.resources.requests) &&
    'memory' in c.resources.requests &&
    c.resources.requests['memory'].asInteger() > 0 &&
    c.resources.limits['memory'].asInteger() * 2 <=
      c.resources.requests['memory'].asInteger() * 5
  )
)
```

The `* 2 <= * 5` avoids floating-point arithmetic while expressing the `≤ 2.5` ratio exactly. The guards mirror the original defaults (`0` for missing limits, `1m` → treated as absent/zero for requests) while avoiding a division-by-zero in integer arithmetic.

input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: inject-sidecar
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Inject Sidecar Container
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Deployment,Volume
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The sidecar pattern is very common in Kubernetes whereby other applications can
      insert components via tacit modification of a submitted resource. This is, for example,
      often how service meshes and secrets applications are able to function transparently.
      This policy injects a sidecar container, initContainer, and volume into Pods that match
      an annotation called `vault.hashicorp.com/agent-inject: true`.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: inject-sidecar
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Deployment
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        spec:
          template:
            metadata:
              annotations:
                (vault.hashicorp.com/agent-inject): "true"
            spec:
              containers:
              - name: vault-agent
                image: vault:1.5.4
                imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
                volumeMounts:
                - mountPath: /vault/secrets
                  name: vault-secret
              initContainers:
              - name: vault-agent-init
                image: vault:1.5.4
                imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
                volumeMounts:
                - mountPath: /vault/secrets
                  name: vault-secret
              volumes:
              - name: vault-secret
                emptyDir:
                  medium: Memory
raw_log:
Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

**Structural changes**

| Old | New |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: MutatingPolicy` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Deployment]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with explicit `apiGroups: ["apps"]`, `apiVersions: ["v1"]`, `resources: ["deployments"]`, `operations: ["CREATE","UPDATE"]` |
| `spec.rules[].mutate.patchStrategicMerge` | `spec.mutations[].patchType: ApplyConfiguration` with CEL |
| `policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0` | `1.16.0` |

**PSM condition anchor → `matchConditions`**

The old PSM anchor `(vault.hashicorp.com/agent-inject): "true"` (parentheses = precondition) is converted to a CEL `matchConditions` entry that guards the entire mutation:
```cel
has(object.spec.template.metadata) &&
has(object.spec.template.metadata.annotations) &&
object.spec.template.metadata.annotations['vault.hashicorp.com/agent-inject'] == 'true'
```

**Mutation body → CEL `applyConfiguration` expression**

The `patchStrategicMerge` payload becomes a CEL `Object{}` expression using typed structural paths (`Object.spec.template.spec.containers`, etc.). Because Kubernetes performs server-side apply with `name` as the merge key for `containers`, `initContainers`, and `volumes`, specifying only the new entries is sufficient — existing containers/volumes are preserved.

input_snippet:
# This is an example rule intended to be cloned & modified to meet organizational requirements.
# The `dataprotetion` label value can be changed to correspond with specific policy templates.
# 
# NOTE: Use of this policy will require granting the Kyverno background-controller additional privileges required to generate Kasten resources. An example ClusterRole to provide required privileges is provided within the comments of the policy manifest.
#
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:
# - apiGroups:
#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-example-backup-policy
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Backup Policy Based on Resource Label
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.12.1
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.12.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24-1.30"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a namespace that includes any Deployment or StatefulSet with a "dataprotection=kasten-example" label, if the policy does not already exist. This Kyverno policy can be used in combination with the "kasten-data-protection-by-label" policy to require "dataprotection" labeling on workloads.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: kasten-generate-example-backup-policy
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
            - Deployment
            - StatefulSet
          selector:
            matchLabels:
              dataprotection: kasten-example
    context:
    - name: dataprotectionLabelValue
      variable:
        value: "kasten-example"
    - name: kyvernoPolicyName
      variable:
        value: "kasten-generate-example-backup-policy"
    - name: existingPolicy
      apiCall:
        urlPath: "/apis/config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1/namespaces/kasten-io/policies" # returns list of Kasten policies from kasten-io namespace
        jmesPath: "items[][[@.metadata.labels.\"generate.kyverno.io/policy-name\"=='{{ kyvernoPolicyName }}'] && [@.spec.selector.matchExpressions[].values[?@=='{{ request.namespace }}']]][][][][] | length(@)" # queries if a Kasten policy protecting the namespace generated by this Kyverno policy already exists 
    preconditions:
      any:
      - key: "{{ existingPolicy }}"
        operator: Equals
        value: 0 # Only generate the policy if it does not already exist
    generate:
      apiVersion: config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1
      kind: Policy
      name: "{{ request.namespace }}-{{ dataprotectionLabelValue }}-policy"
      namespace: kasten-io
      data:   
        metadata:

Written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

| Old (ClusterPolicy) | New (GeneratingPolicy) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1alpha1` |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: GeneratingPolicy` |
| `kyverno.io/kyverno-version`, `kyverno.io/kubernetes-version` annotations | Removed (not used in new format) |
| `policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.12.0` | `policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any` with `kinds: [Deployment, StatefulSet]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` → `apps/v1` `deployments`+`statefulsets`, operations `CREATE`+`UPDATE` |
| `match.any[].resources.selector.matchLabels` | `spec.matchConditions` CEL expression checking `object.metadata.labels['dataprotection'] == 'kasten-example'` |
| `context` variables (`dataprotectionLabelValue`, `kyvernoPolicyName`) + `apiCall` (`existingPolicy`) + `preconditions` (`existingPolicy == 0`) | Variables inlined into the `downstream` CEL expression; existence check dropped in favor of generator idempotency (no `synchronize` enabled means the resource is created once and not re-reconciled) |
| `spec.rules[].generate` with JMESPath `{{ request.namespace }}` interpolation | `spec.variables[].downstream` CEL list with `dyn()` wrappers; `generator.Apply("kasten-io", variables.downstream)` targets the `kasten-io` namespace |

input_snippet:
# This example assumes that Kasten policy presets named "gold", "silver", and "bronze" have been pre-created and Kasten was deployed into the `kasten-io` namespace.
#
# NOTE: Use of this policy will require granting the Kyverno background-controller additional privileges required to generate Kasten resources. An example ClusterRole to provide required privileges is provided within the comments of the policy manifest.
#
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:

#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-policy-by-preset-label
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Policy from Preset
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.12.1
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.12.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24-1.30"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a new namespace that includes a valid "dataprotection" label, if the policy does not already exist.
      Use with "kasten-validate-ns-by-preset-label" policy to require "dataprotection" labeling on new namespaces.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: kasten-generate-policy-by-preset-label
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
            - Namespace
          selector:
            matchExpressions:
              - key: dataprotection 
                operator: In
                values: 
                - gold
                - silver
                - bronze
    context:
    - name: existingPolicy
      apiCall:
        urlPath: "/apis/config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1/namespaces/kasten-io/policies" # returns list of Kasten policies from kasten-io namespace
        jmesPath: "items[][[@.spec.presetRef][?name=='{{ request.object.metadata.labels.dataprotection }}'] && [@.spec.selector.matchExpressions[].values[?@=='{{ request.namespace }}']]][][][][] | length(@)" # queries if a policy based on the dataprotection label value, covering that app namespace already exists 
    preconditions:
      any:
      - key: "{{ existingPolicy }}"
        operator: Equals
        value: 0 # Only generate the policy if it does not already exist
    generate:
      apiVersion: config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1
      kind: Policy
      name: "{{ request.namespace }}-{{ request.object.metadata.labels.dataprotection }}-backup"
      namespace: kasten-io
      data:
        spec:
          comment: "Auto-generated by Kyverno"
          paused: false
          actions:
            - action: backup
          presetRef:
            name: "{{ request.object.metadata.labels.dataprotection }}"
            namespace: kasten-io
          selector:
            matchExpressions:
              - key: k10.kasten.io/appNamespace
                operator: In
                values:
                  - "{{ request.namespace }}"

# This example assumes that Kasten policy presets named "gold", "silver", and "bronze" have been pre-created and Kasten was deployed into the `kasten-io` namespace.
#
# NOTE: Use of this policy will require granting the Kyverno background-controller additional privileges required to generate Kasten resources. An example ClusterRole to provide required privileges is provided within the comments of the policy manifest.
#
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:
# - apiGroups:
#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1alpha1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-policy-by-preset-label
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Policy from Preset
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a new namespace that includes a valid "dataprotection" label, if the policy does not already exist.
      Use with "kasten-validate-ns-by-preset-label" policy to require "dataprotection" labeling on new namespaces.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE"]
        resources: ["namespaces"]
  matchConditions:
    - name: has-valid-dataprotection-label
      expression: >-
        has(object.metadata.labels) &&
        'dataprotection' in object.metadata.labels &&
        object.metadata.labels['dataprotection'] in ['gold', 'silver', 'bronze']
    - name: no-existing-policy
      expression: >-
        http.Get('/apis/config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1/namespaces/kasten-io/policies').body.items.filter(p,
          has(p.spec.presetRef) && p.spec.presetRef.name == object.metadata.labels['dataprotection'] &&
          has(p.spec.selector) && has(p.spec.selector.matchExpressions) &&
          p.spec.selector.matchExpressions.exists(e,
            has(e.values) && e.values.exists(v, v == object.metadata.name)
          )
        ).size() == 0
  variables:
    - name: namespace
      expression: "object.metadata.name"
    - name: preset
      expression: "object.metadata.labels['dataprotection']"
    - name: downstream
      expression: >-
        [
          {
            "kind": dyn("Policy"),
            "apiVersion": dyn("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name": variables.namespace + '-' + variables.preset + '-backup',
              "namespace": "kasten-io"
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "comment": "Auto-generated by Kyverno",
              "paused": false,
              "actions": dyn([
                {"action": dyn("backup")}
              ]),
              "presetRef": dyn({
                "name": variables.preset,
                "namespace": "kasten-io"
              }),
              "selector": dyn({
                "matchExpressions": dyn([
                  {
                    "key": dyn("k10.kasten.io/appNamespace"),
                    "operator": dyn("In"),
                    "values": dyn([variables.namespace])
                  }
                ])
              })
            })
          }
        ]
  generate:
    - expression: "generator.Apply('kasten-io', variables.downstream)"
raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

**Structural changes:**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1alpha1` / `kind: GeneratingPolicy`
- `policies.kyverno.io/minversion` updated to `1.16.0`, `kyverno.io/kyverno-version` dropped (not used in new format)

**`spec.rules[].match` → `spec.matchConstraints`:**
- `kinds: [Namespace]` mapped to `resourceRules` with `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: ["v1"]`, `resources: ["namespaces"]`, `operations: ["CREATE"]`

**`selector.matchExpressions` + `preconditions` → `spec.matchConditions` (CEL):**
- `has-valid-dataprotection-label`: replaces the old `matchExpressions` label selector, checking the `dataprotection` label is one of `gold`, `silver`, or `bronze`
- `no-existing-policy`: replaces both the old `context.apiCall` (JMESPath) and the `preconditions` block — uses `http.Get()` to fetch existing Kasten policies and filters them with CEL to assert none already match this preset + namespace combination

**`generate` block → `spec.variables` + `spec.generate`:**
- `variables.namespace` / `variables.preset` extract `object.metadata.name` and the label value (replacing `{{ request.namespace }}` and `{{ request.object.metadata.labels.dataprotection }}`)
- `variables.downstream` builds the Kasten `Policy` resource using `dyn()` wrappers for nested objects/arrays, following the reference pattern
- `generate[].expression` calls `generator.Apply('kasten-io', variables.downstream)` targeting the `kasten-io` namespace

description: this policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific ConfigMaps by ServiceAccounts
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_limit_configmap_for_sa.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy this policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific ConfigMaps by ServiceAccounts. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_limit_configmap_for_sa.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: limit-configmap-for-sa
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Limit ConfigMap to ServiceAccounts for a User
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.20-1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: ConfigMap, ServiceAccount
    policies.kyverno.io/description: This policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific ConfigMaps by ServiceAccounts.
spec:
  background: false
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  rules:
  - name: limit-configmap-for-sa-developer
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - ConfigMap
        # subjects:
        # - kind: ServiceAccount
        #   name: developer
        #   namespace: kube-system
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - ConfigMap
        subjects:
        - kind: ServiceAccount
          name: another-developer
          namespace: another-namespace
    preconditions:
      all:
      - key: "{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}"
        operator: AnyIn
        value:
        - "any-namespace"
        - "another-namespace"
      - key: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
        operator: AnyIn
        value:
        - "any-configmap-name-good"
        - "another-configmap-name"
    validate:
      message: "{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}/{{request.object.kind}}/{{request.object.metadata.name}} resource is protected. Admin or allowed users can change the resource"
      deny:
        conditions:
          all:
          - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
            operator: AnyIn
            value:
            - UPDATE
            - CREATE
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: limit-configmap-for-sa
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Limit ConfigMap to ServiceAccounts for a User
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: ConfigMap, ServiceAccount
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      This policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific
      ConfigMaps by ServiceAccounts.
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ''
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - configmaps
  matchConditions:
    - name: target-namespace
      expression: >-
        object.metadata.namespace in ['any-namespace', 'another-namespace']
    - name: target-configmap-name
      expression: >-
        object.metadata.name in ['any-configmap-name-good', 'another-configmap-name']
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: "false"
      messageExpression: >-
        object.metadata.namespace + '/' + object.kind + '/' +
        object.metadata.name + ' resource is protected. Admin or allowed users can change the resource'
raw_log:
The converted policy is at `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every change made:

| Old (ClusterPolicy) | New (ValidatingPolicy) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` | New API group |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` | New kind |
| `kyverno.io/kyverno-version` annotation | dropped | Version-specific, not part of new schema |
| `kyverno.io/kubernetes-version` annotation | dropped | Same |
| `spec.background: false` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: false` | Structural move |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` | Renamed, now a list |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[0]` (ConfigMap, any requester) | `matchConstraints.resourceRules` — ConfigMaps, CREATE/UPDATE | Core group `''`, resource `configmaps` (lowercase plural) |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[1]` (ConfigMap + SA subjects) | dropped | Subsumed by entry [0] which already matches all requesters |
| `preconditions.all` (namespace + name filters) | `matchConditions` (two CEL expressions) | JMESPath `AnyIn` → CEL `in` list membership |
| `validate.deny.conditions` (operation in CREATE/UPDATE) | operations already in `resourceRules`; `validations[].expression: "false"` | Deny logic inverted: "always deny" when scoped match is met |
| `validate.message` (Kyverno JMESPath template) | `validations[].messageExpression` (CEL string concat) | `{{request.object.kind}}` → `object.kind`; `{{request.object.metadata.*}}` → `object.metadata.*` |

description: when a Pod controller which can run multiple replicas is subject to an active PodDisruptionBudget, if the replicas field has a value equal to the minAvailable value of the PodDisruptionBudget it may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains which may impact routine maintenance tasks and disrupt operations
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_pdb_minavailable.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy when a Pod controller which can run multiple replicas is subject to an active PodDisruptionBudget, if the replicas field has a value equal to the minAvailable value of the PodDisruptionBudget it may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains which may impact routine maintenance tasks and disrupt operations. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_pdb_minavailable.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: pdb-minavailable-check
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Check PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.9.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: PodDisruptionBudget, Deployment, StatefulSet
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      When a Pod controller which can run multiple replicas is subject to an active PodDisruptionBudget,
      if the replicas field has a value equal to the minAvailable value of the PodDisruptionBudget
      it may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains which may impact routine maintenance
      tasks and disrupt operations. This policy checks incoming Deployments and StatefulSets which have
      a matching PodDisruptionBudget to ensure these two values do not match.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: false
  rules:
    - name: pdb-minavailable
      match:
        any:
          - resources:
              kinds:
                - Deployment
                - StatefulSet
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: AnyIn
          value:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        - key: "{{ request.object.spec.replicas || `1` }}"
          operator: GreaterThan
          value: 0
      context:
        - name: minavailable
          apiCall:
            urlPath: "/apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{{request.namespace}}/poddisruptionbudgets"
            jmesPath: "items[?label_match(spec.selector.matchLabels, `{{request.object.spec.template.metadata.labels}}`)] | [0] | spec.minAvailable || `0`"
      validate:
        message: >-
          The matching PodDisruptionBudget for this resource has its minAvailable value equal to the replica count
          which is not permitted.
        deny:
          conditions:
            any:
              - key: "{{ request.object.spec.replicas }}"
                operator: Equals
                value: "{{ minavailable }}"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: pdb-minavailable-check
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Check PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: PodDisruptionBudget, Deployment, StatefulSet
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      When a Pod controller which can run multiple replicas is subject to an active PodDisruptionBudget,
      if the replicas field has a value equal to the minAvailable value of the PodDisruptionBudget
      it may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains which may impact routine maintenance
      tasks and disrupt operations. This policy checks incoming Deployments and StatefulSets which have
      a matching PodDisruptionBudget to ensure these two values do not match.
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
          - statefulsets
  matchConditions:
    - name: replicas-greater-than-zero
      expression: >-
        (has(object.spec.replicas) ? object.spec.replicas : 1) > 0
  variables:
    - name: pdbs
      expression: >-
        http.Get("/apis/policy/v1/namespaces/" + object.metadata.namespace + "/poddisruptionbudgets")
    - name: matchingPdbs
      expression: >-
        variables.pdbs.items.filter(pdb,
          has(pdb.spec.selector) && has(pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels) &&
          has(object.spec.template.metadata.labels) &&
          pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels.all(k,
            k in object.spec.template.metadata.labels &&
            object.spec.template.metadata.labels[k] == pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels[k]
          )
        )
    - name: minAvailable
      expression: >-
        variables.matchingPdbs.size() > 0 && has(variables.matchingPdbs[0].spec.minAvailable) ?
        int(variables.matchingPdbs[0].spec.minAvailable) : 0
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.minAvailable == 0 ||
        (has(object.spec.replicas) ? object.spec.replicas : 1) != variables.minAvailable
      message: >-
        The matching PodDisruptionBudget for this resource has its minAvailable value equal to the replica count
        which is not permitted.
raw_log:
Here's a summary of the conversion decisions:

**Structural changes:**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ValidatingPolicy`
- `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` → `spec.validationActions: [Audit]`
- `spec.background: false` → `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: false`

**Match rules:**
- `match.any[].resources.kinds: [Deployment, StatefulSet]` → `matchConstraints.resourceRules` with explicit `apiGroups: [apps]`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, `resources: [deployments, statefulsets]` (lowercase, plural)
- Precondition for operation (CREATE/UPDATE) is now expressed directly in `matchConstraints.resourceRules.operations`
- Precondition for `replicas > 0` (with default of 1) becomes a `matchConditions` entry using a CEL ternary

**Context → Variables:**
- The `context.apiCall` for fetching PDBs becomes a `variables` entry using `http.Get()`
- The JMESPath `label_match` + filter logic is rewritten as a CEL `filter()` expression comparing `matchLabels` against pod template labels
- `minAvailable` defaults to `0` when no matching PDB is found (same semantics as the original `|| \`0\``)

**Validation logic:**
- The `validate.deny.conditions` (deny when `replicas == minAvailable`) is inverted into a CEL `validations.expression` that **passes** when `minAvailable == 0` (no matching PDB) OR when replicas differ from minAvailable

description: capabilities permit privileged actions without giving full root access
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_drop_all.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy capabilities permit privileged actions without giving full root access. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_require_drop_all.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: drop-all-capabilities
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Drop All Capabilities
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Capabilities permit privileged actions without giving full root access. All
      capabilities should be dropped from a Pod, with only those required added back.
      This policy ensures that all containers explicitly specify the `drop: ["ALL"]`
      ability. Note that this policy also illustrates how to cover drop entries in any
      case although this may not strictly conform to the Pod Security Standards.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
    - name: require-drop-all
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Pod
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{ request.operation || 'BACKGROUND' }}"
          operator: NotEquals
          value: DELETE
      validate:
        message: >-
          Containers must drop `ALL` capabilities.
        foreach:
          - list: request.object.spec.[ephemeralContainers, initContainers, containers][]
            deny:
              conditions:
                all:
                - key: ALL
                  operator: AnyNotIn
                  value: "{{ element.securityContext.capabilities.drop[].to_upper(@) || `[]` }}"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: drop-all-capabilities
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Drop All Capabilities
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Capabilities permit privileged actions without giving full root access. All
      capabilities should be dropped from a Pod, with only those required added back.
      This policy ensures that all containers explicitly specify the `drop: ["ALL"]`
      ability. Note that this policy also illustrates how to cover drop entries in any
      case although this may not strictly conform to the Pod Security Standards.
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ''
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(c,
          has(c.securityContext) &&
          has(c.securityContext.capabilities) &&
          has(c.securityContext.capabilities.drop) &&
          c.securityContext.capabilities.drop.exists(d, d.upperAscii() == 'ALL')
        ) &&
        (!has(object.spec.initContainers) || object.spec.initContainers.all(c,
          has(c.securityContext) &&
          has(c.securityContext.capabilities) &&
          has(c.securityContext.capabilities.drop) &&
          c.securityContext.capabilities.drop.exists(d, d.upperAscii() == 'ALL')
        )) &&
        (!has(object.spec.ephemeralContainers) || object.spec.ephemeralContainers.all(c,
          has(c.securityContext) &&
          has(c.securityContext.capabilities) &&
          has(c.securityContext.capabilities.drop) &&
          c.securityContext.capabilities.drop.exists(d, d.upperAscii() == 'ALL')
        ))
      messageExpression: >-
        'Pod ' + object.metadata.name + ': all containers must explicitly drop ALL capabilities via securityContext.capabilities.drop.'
raw_log:
Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

| Old (ClusterPolicy) | New (ValidatingPolicy) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy` |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `spec.background: true` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with explicit `apiGroups`, `apiVersions`, `resources`, `operations` |
| `preconditions` excluding DELETE | Dropped — `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` already excludes DELETE |
| `validate.foreach` + JMESPath `deny` with `AnyNotIn` | CEL `validations[].expression` using `all()` macro per container list |
| JMESPath `to_upper(@)` + `AnyNotIn "ALL"` | CEL `.upperAscii() == 'ALL'` inside `exists()` |
| `validate.message` (static) | `messageExpression` (dynamic, includes pod name) |

The `foreach` over all three container lists (containers/initContainers/ephemeralContainers) becomes three separate `all()` clauses in the CEL expression, with `has()` guards on `initContainers` and `ephemeralContainers` since those are optional fields.

description: define and use labels that identify semantic attributes of your application or Deployment
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_labels.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy define and use labels that identify semantic attributes of your application or Deployment. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_require_labels.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Labels
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Label
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Define and use labels that identify semantic attributes of your application or Deployment.
      A common set of labels allows tools to work collaboratively, describing objects in a common manner that
      all tools can understand. The recommended labels describe applications in a way that can be
      queried. This policy validates that the label `app.kubernetes.io/name` is specified with some value.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: check-for-labels
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "The label `app.kubernetes.io/name` is required."
      pattern:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app.kubernetes.io/name: "?*"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Enforce the presence of required labels that identify semantic attributes
      of applications or Deployments. Specifically, ensure that the standard
      label `app.kubernetes.io/name` is present on Pods with a non-empty value
      to enable consistent tooling and querying capabilities.
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Labels
  name: require-labels
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ''
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        has(object.metadata.labels) && 'app.kubernetes.io/name' in
        object.metadata.labels &&
        object.metadata.labels['app.kubernetes.io/name'] != ''
      messageExpression: >-
        'Pod ' + object.metadata.name + ' must have the label
        app.kubernetes.io/name with a non-empty value. Found labels: ' +
        (has(object.metadata.labels) ? string(object.metadata.labels) : 'none')
raw_log:
Here's a summary of the key changes made during conversion:

| Aspect | ClusterPolicy (old) | ValidatingPolicy (new) |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion` | `kyverno.io/v1` | `policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind` | `ClusterPolicy` | `ValidatingPolicy` |
| `validationFailureAction: Audit` | top-level in `spec` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `background: true` | top-level in `spec` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true` |
| `match.any[].resources.kinds` | `- Pod` (short form) | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with explicit `apiGroups`, `apiVersions`, `operations`, and `resources: [pods]` |
| Validation logic | `validate.pattern` (Kyverno pattern syntax) | `spec.validations[].expression` (CEL expression) |
| Error message | `validate.message` (static string) | `spec.validations[].messageExpression` (dynamic CEL string expression) |
| `minversion` annotation | kept | removed (not relevant to new API) |
| `subject` annotation | kept | removed (not used in new API) |

The pattern `app.kubernetes.io/name: "?*"` was translated to the CEL expression that checks the label exists and is non-empty, and the static message was upgraded to a dynamic `messageExpression` that includes the pod name and actual labels for better debuggability.

description: podDisruptionBudget resources are useful to ensuring minimum availability is maintained at all times
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_pdb.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy podDisruptionBudget resources are useful to ensuring minimum availability is maintained at all times. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_require_pdb.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-pdb
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require PodDisruptionBudget
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.2
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Deployment, PodDisruptionBudget
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      PodDisruptionBudget resources are useful to ensuring minimum availability
      is maintained at all times. This policy checks all incoming Deployments and StatefulSets
      to ensure they have a matching, preexisting PodDisruptionBudget.
      Note: This policy must be run in `enforce` mode to ensure accuracy.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: false
  rules:
    - name: require-pdb
      match:
        any:
          - resources:
              kinds:
                - Deployment
                - StatefulSet
      preconditions:
        all:
          - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
            operator: Equals
            value: CREATE
          - key: "{{ request.object.spec.replicas }}"
            operator: GreaterThanOrEquals
            value: 3
      context:
        - name: pdb_count
          apiCall:
            urlPath: "/apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{{request.namespace}}/poddisruptionbudgets"
            jmesPath: "items[?label_match(spec.selector.matchLabels, `{{request.object.spec.template.metadata.labels}}`)] | length(@)"
      validate:
        message: "There is no corresponding PodDisruptionBudget found for this Deployment."
        deny:
          conditions:
            any:
              - key: "{{pdb_count}}"
                operator: LessThan
                value: 1
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-pdb
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require PodDisruptionBudget
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Deployment, PodDisruptionBudget
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      PodDisruptionBudget resources are useful to ensuring minimum availability
      is maintained at all times. This policy checks all incoming Deployments and StatefulSets
      to ensure they have a matching, preexisting PodDisruptionBudget.
      Note: This policy must be run in `enforce` mode to ensure accuracy.
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
        resources:
          - deployments
          - statefulsets
  matchConditions:
    - name: replicas-gte-3
      expression: >-
        has(object.spec.replicas) && object.spec.replicas >= 3
  variables:
    - name: pdbs
      expression: >-
        http.Get('/apis/policy/v1/namespaces/' + object.metadata.namespace + '/poddisruptionbudgets').body.items
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.pdbs.exists(pdb,
          has(pdb.spec.selector) &&
          has(pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels) &&
          pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels.all(k,
            has(object.spec.template.metadata.labels) &&
            k in object.spec.template.metadata.labels &&
            object.spec.template.metadata.labels[k] == pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels[k]
          )
        )
      message: "There is no corresponding PodDisruptionBudget found for this Deployment."
raw_log:
The converted policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every conversion made:

| Old (ClusterPolicy) | New (ValidatingPolicy) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` |
| Dropped `kyverno.io/kyverno-version`, `kyverno.io/kubernetes-version`, `minversion` annotations | (per reference pattern) |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `spec.background: false` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: false` |
| `match.any[].resources.kinds: [Deployment, StatefulSet]` | `matchConstraints.resourceRules` — `apps/v1`, resources: `deployments` + `statefulsets` |
| Precondition `operation == CREATE` | Only `CREATE` in `resourceRules[].operations` |
| Precondition `replicas >= 3` (JMESPath) | `matchConditions` CEL: `has(object.spec.replicas) && object.spec.replicas >= 3` |
| `context[].apiCall` with JMESPath `label_match` | `variables[].expression` using `http.Get(...)` returning `.body.items` |
| `deny` if `pdb_count < 1` | `validations[].expression` positive CEL assertion: `variables.pdbs.exists(pdb, ...)` checking all PDB `matchLabels` keys are present and match in the pod template labels |

description: liveness and readiness probes need to be configured to correctly manage a Pod's lifecycle during deployments, restarts, and upgrades
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_probes.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy liveness and readiness probes need to be configured to correctly manage a Pod's lifecycle during deployments, restarts, and upgrades. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_require_probes.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-pod-probes
  annotations:
    pod-policies.kyverno.io/autogen-controllers: DaemonSet,Deployment,StatefulSet
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Pod Probes
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Liveness and readiness probes need to be configured to correctly manage a Pod's
      lifecycle during deployments, restarts, and upgrades. For each Pod, a periodic
      `livenessProbe` is performed by the kubelet to determine if the Pod's containers
      are running or need to be restarted. A `readinessProbe` is used by Services
      and Deployments to determine if the Pod is ready to receive network traffic.
      This policy validates that all containers have one of livenessProbe, readinessProbe,
      or startupProbe defined.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-probes
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    preconditions:
      all:
      - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
        operator: AnyIn
        value:
        - CREATE
        - UPDATE
    validate:
      message: "Liveness, readiness, or startup probes are required for all containers."
      foreach:
      - list: request.object.spec.containers[]
        deny:
          conditions:
            all:
            - key: livenessProbe
              operator: AllNotIn
              value: "{{ element.keys(@)[] }}"
            - key: startupProbe
              operator: AllNotIn
              value: "{{ element.keys(@)[] }}"
            - key: readinessProbe
              operator: AllNotIn
              value: "{{ element.keys(@)[] }}"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-pod-probes
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Pod Probes
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Liveness and readiness probes need to be configured to correctly manage a Pod's
      lifecycle during deployments, restarts, and upgrades. For each Pod, a periodic
      `livenessProbe` is performed by the kubelet to determine if the Pod's containers
      are running or need to be restarted. A `readinessProbe` is used by Services
      and Deployments to determine if the Pod is ready to receive network traffic.
      This policy validates that all containers have one of livenessProbe, readinessProbe,
      or startupProbe defined.
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ''
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(c,
          has(c.livenessProbe) || has(c.startupProbe) || has(c.readinessProbe))
      messageExpression: >-
        'Liveness, readiness, or startup probes are required for all containers. Container(s) missing probes: ' +
        object.spec.containers.filter(c,
          !has(c.livenessProbe) && !has(c.startupProbe) && !has(c.readinessProbe)
        ).map(c, c.name).join(', ')
raw_log:
The converted policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every change made:

| Old (ClusterPolicy) | New (ValidatingPolicy) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` |
| `pod-policies.kyverno.io/autogen-controllers` annotation | Removed (autogen is native in 1.16+) |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `spec.background: true` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true` |
| `spec.rules[].match` + `preconditions` (CREATE/UPDATE) | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` and `apiGroups/apiVersions/resources` filled in for Pods |
| `validate.foreach` + `deny` with JMESPath `element.keys(@)[]` operators | `spec.validations[].expression` as a CEL expression: `object.spec.containers.all(c, has(c.livenessProbe) \|\| has(c.startupProbe) \|\| has(c.readinessProbe))` |
| Static `validate.message` string | `messageExpression` that dynamically names the offending container(s) |

The core logic translation: the original `deny` fires when all three probes are `AllNotIn` the container's keys — meaning the container has none of the three probes. The CEL equivalent asserts the positive: every container must have at least one probe (`has(c.livenessProbe) || has(c.startupProbe) || has(c.readinessProbe)`).

description: as application workloads share cluster resources, it is important to limit resources requested and consumed by each Pod
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_resource_limits.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy as application workloads share cluster resources, it is important to limit resources requested and consumed by each Pod. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_require_resource_limits.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-requests-limits
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Limits and Requests
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      As application workloads share cluster resources, it is important to limit resources
      requested and consumed by each Pod. It is recommended to require resource requests and
      limits per Pod, especially for memory and CPU. If a Namespace level request or limit is specified,
      defaults will automatically be applied to each Pod based on the LimitRange configuration.
      This policy validates that all containers have something specified for memory and CPU
      requests and memory limits.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-resources
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "CPU and memory resource requests and memory limits are required for containers."
      pattern:
        spec:
          containers:
          - resources:
              requests:
                memory: "?*"
                cpu: "?*"
              limits:
                memory: "?*"
          =(initContainers):
          - resources:
              requests:
                memory: "?*"
                cpu: "?*"
              limits:
                memory: "?*"
          =(ephemeralContainers):
          - resources:
              requests:
                memory: "?*"
                cpu: "?*"
              limits:
                memory: "?*"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      As application workloads share cluster resources, it is important to limit
      resources requested and consumed by each Pod. It is recommended to require
      resource requests and limits per Pod, especially for memory and CPU. If a
      Namespace level request or limit is specified, defaults will automatically
      be applied to each Pod based on the LimitRange configuration. This policy
      validates that all containers have something specified for memory and CPU
      requests and memory limits.
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Limits and Requests
  name: require-requests-limits
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ''
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(c,
          has(c.resources) &&
          has(c.resources.requests) &&
          has(c.resources.requests.memory) &&
          has(c.resources.requests.cpu) &&
          has(c.resources.limits) &&
          has(c.resources.limits.memory)
        ) &&
        (!has(object.spec.initContainers) || object.spec.initContainers.all(c,
          has(c.resources) &&
          has(c.resources.requests) &&
          has(c.resources.requests.memory) &&
          has(c.resources.requests.cpu) &&
          has(c.resources.limits) &&
          has(c.resources.limits.memory)
        )) &&
        (!has(object.spec.ephemeralContainers) || object.spec.ephemeralContainers.all(c,
          has(c.resources) &&
          has(c.resources.requests) &&
          has(c.resources.requests.memory) &&
          has(c.resources.requests.cpu) &&
          has(c.resources.limits) &&
          has(c.resources.limits.memory)
        ))
      messageExpression: >-
        'Pod ' + object.metadata.name + ' must define CPU and memory requests
        and memory limits for all containers (including initContainers and
        ephemeralContainers if present).'
raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every conversion applied:

| Old (ClusterPolicy) | New (ValidatingPolicy) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `spec.background: true` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true` |
| `match.any[].resources.kinds: [Pod]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with `apiGroups: ['']`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]`, `resources: [pods]` |
| `validate.pattern` (Kyverno pattern syntax) | `spec.validations[].expression` (CEL) |
| `validate.message` (static string) | `spec.validations[].messageExpression` (CEL string expression) |
| `minversion` / `subject` annotations | Dropped (not carried in new API) |

**CEL expression logic:**
- **`containers`** (always required): `all()` checks that each container has `resources.requests.{memory,cpu}` and `resources.limits.memory`
- **`=(initContainers)` / `=(ephemeralContainers)`** (optional in old syntax): translated to `!has(...) || ...all(...)` — if the field is absent the check passes; if present, every container must comply

description: a read-only root file system helps to enforce an immutable infrastructure strategy; the container only needs to write on the mounted volume that persists the state
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_ro_rootfs.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy a read-only root file system helps to enforce an immutable infrastructure strategy; the container only needs to write on the mounted volume that persists the state. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_require_ro_rootfs.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-ro-rootfs
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Read-Only Root Filesystem
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices, PSP Migration
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A read-only root file system helps to enforce an immutable infrastructure strategy;
      the container only needs to write on the mounted volume that persists the state.
      An immutable root filesystem can also prevent malicious binaries from writing to the
      host system. This policy validates that containers define a securityContext
      with `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-readOnlyRootFilesystem
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Root filesystem must be read-only."
      pattern:
        spec:
          containers:
          - securityContext:
              readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices, PSP Migration
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A read-only root file system helps to enforce an immutable infrastructure
      strategy; the container only needs to write on the mounted volume that
      persists the state. An immutable root filesystem can also prevent malicious
      binaries from writing to the host system. This policy validates that
      containers define a securityContext with `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`.
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Read-Only Root Filesystem
  name: require-ro-rootfs
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ''
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(c,
          has(c.securityContext) &&
          has(c.securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem) &&
          c.securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem == true)
      messageExpression: >-
        'Pod ' + object.metadata.name + ': root filesystem must be read-only. ' +
        'Container(s) missing readOnlyRootFilesystem: true — ' +
        object.spec.containers.filter(c,
          !has(c.securityContext) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem) ||
          c.securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem != true
        ).map(c, c.name).join(', ')
raw_log:
Here's a summary of the key changes made in the conversion:

**API / Kind**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1`
- `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `kind: ValidatingPolicy`

**Metadata**
- Annotations reordered alphabetically; `policies.kyverno.io/minversion` and `policies.kyverno.io/subject` dropped (not carried over in the new format, per reference examples).

**Spec structure**
| Old | New |
|---|---|
| `validationFailureAction: Audit` | `validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `background: true` | `evaluation.background.enabled: true` |
| `rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Pod]` | `matchConstraints.resourceRules` with explicit `apiGroups: ['']`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]`, `resources: [pods]` |
| `rules[].validate.pattern` (Kyverno pattern) | `validations[].expression` (CEL expression) |
| `rules[].validate.message` (static string) | `validations[].messageExpression` (CEL string expression) |

**Validation logic**

The original Kyverno pattern `containers[*].securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem: true` is expressed in CEL as:
```cel
object.spec.containers.all(c,
  has(c.securityContext) &&
  has(c.securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem) &&
  c.securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem == true)
```

The `messageExpression` also dynamically lists the offending container names to aid debugging.

description: adding capabilities is a way for containers in a Pod to request higher levels of ability than those with which they may be provisioned
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_restrict_capabilities.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy adding capabilities is a way for containers in a Pod to request higher levels of ability than those with which they may be provisioned. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_restrict_capabilities.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: psp-restrict-adding-capabilities
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Adding Capabilities
    policies.kyverno.io/category: PSP Migration
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Adding capabilities is a way for containers in a Pod to request higher levels
      of ability than those with which they may be provisioned. Many capabilities
      allow system-level control and should be prevented. Pod Security Policies (PSP)
      allowed a list of "good" capabilities to be added. This policy checks
      ephemeralContainers, initContainers, and containers to ensure the only
      capabilities that can be added are either NET_BIND_SERVICE or CAP_CHOWN.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
    - name: allowed-capabilities
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Pod
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{ request.operation || 'BACKGROUND' }}"
          operator: NotEquals
          value: DELETE
      validate:
        message: >-
          Any capabilities added other than NET_BIND_SERVICE or CAP_CHOWN are disallowed.
        foreach:
          - list: request.object.spec.[ephemeralContainers, initContainers, containers][]
            deny:
              conditions:
                all:
                - key: "{{ element.securityContext.capabilities.add[] || '' }}"
                  operator: AnyNotIn
                  value:
                  - NET_BIND_SERVICE
                  - CAP_CHOWN
                  - ''
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: psp-restrict-adding-capabilities
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Adding Capabilities
    policies.kyverno.io/category: PSP Migration
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Adding capabilities is a way for containers in a Pod to request higher levels
      of ability than those with which they may be provisioned. Many capabilities
      allow system-level control and should be prevented. Pod Security Policies (PSP)
      allowed a list of "good" capabilities to be added. This policy checks
      ephemeralContainers, initContainers, and containers to ensure the only
      capabilities that can be added are either NET_BIND_SERVICE or CAP_CHOWN.
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ''
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(c,
          !has(c.securityContext) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.capabilities) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) ||
          c.securityContext.capabilities.add.all(cap, cap in ['NET_BIND_SERVICE', 'CAP_CHOWN'])
        ) &&
        (!has(object.spec.initContainers) || object.spec.initContainers.all(c,
          !has(c.securityContext) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.capabilities) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) ||
          c.securityContext.capabilities.add.all(cap, cap in ['NET_BIND_SERVICE', 'CAP_CHOWN'])
        )) &&
        (!has(object.spec.ephemeralContainers) || object.spec.ephemeralContainers.all(c,
          !has(c.securityContext) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.capabilities) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) ||
          c.securityContext.capabilities.add.all(cap, cap in ['NET_BIND_SERVICE', 'CAP_CHOWN'])
        ))
      message: >-
        Any capabilities added other than NET_BIND_SERVICE or CAP_CHOWN are disallowed.

The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key changes made:

**Structural changes:**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1`
- `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `kind: ValidatingPolicy`

**Spec changes:**
- `spec.background: true` → `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true`
- `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` → `spec.validationActions: [Audit]`
- `spec.rules[].match` → `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` — with explicit `apiGroups: ['']`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, `resources: [pods]`
- The `preconditions` excluding `DELETE` is replaced by listing only `CREATE` and `UPDATE` in `operations`

**Validation logic:**
- The `validate.foreach` + `deny.conditions` pattern (Kyverno JMESPath expressions) is replaced with a single CEL `expression` in `spec.validations`
- The CEL expression checks all three container types (`containers`, `initContainers`, `ephemeralContainers`) and verifies that any added capabilities are only `NET_BIND_SERVICE` or `CAP_CHOWN`, using safe `has()` guards for optional fields

**Annotations dropped:**
- `kyverno.io/kyverno-version`, `policies.kyverno.io/minversion`, `kyverno.io/kubernetes-version`, and `policies.kyverno.io/subject` (version/tooling metadata not used in the new API)

description: images from unknown, public registries can be of dubious quality and may not be scanned and secured, representing a high degree of risk
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_restrict_image_registries.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy images from unknown, public registries can be of dubious quality and may not be scanned and secured, representing a high degree of risk. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_restrict_image_registries.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-image-registries
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Image Registries
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.26"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Images from unknown, public registries can be of dubious quality and may not be
      scanned and secured, representing a high degree of risk. Requiring use of known, approved
      registries helps reduce threat exposure by ensuring image pulls only come from them. This
      policy validates that container images only originate from the registry `eu.foo.io` or
      `bar.io`. Use of this policy requires customization to define your allowable registries.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-registries
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Unknown image registry."
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(ephemeralContainers):
          - image: "eu.foo.io/* | bar.io/*"
          =(initContainers):
          - image: "eu.foo.io/* | bar.io/*"
          containers:
          - image: "eu.foo.io/* | bar.io/*"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Images from unknown, public registries can be of dubious quality and may
      not be scanned and secured, representing a high degree of risk. This
      policy validates that all container images (including init and ephemeral
      containers) only originate from the approved registries `eu.foo.io` or
      `bar.io`. Customize the allowed registry prefixes to match your
      environment.
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Image Registries
  name: restrict-image-registries
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ''
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(c, c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') ||
        c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')) &&
        (!has(object.spec.initContainers) ||
        object.spec.initContainers.all(c, c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') ||
        c.image.startsWith('bar.io/'))) &&
        (!has(object.spec.ephemeralContainers) ||
        object.spec.ephemeralContainers.all(c, c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') ||
        c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')))
      messageExpression: >-
        'Unknown image registry. All images must originate from eu.foo.io or
        bar.io. Offending images: ' +
        (object.spec.containers +
        (has(object.spec.initContainers) ? object.spec.initContainers : []) +
        (has(object.spec.ephemeralContainers) ? object.spec.ephemeralContainers : [])
        ).filter(c, !c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') &&
        !c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')).map(c, c.image).join(', ')

**API/Kind**
