- `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `kind: ValidatingPolicy`

**Metadata**
- Removed `policies.kyverno.io/minversion`, `kyverno.io/kubernetes-version`, and `policies.kyverno.io/subject` annotations (not used in the new API)
- Rewritten description to be more concise

**Spec structure**
- `background: true` → `evaluation.background.enabled: true`
- `validationFailureAction: Audit` → `validationActions: [Audit]`
- `rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Pod]` → `matchConstraints.resourceRules` with explicit `apiGroups: ['']`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]`, `resources: [pods]`

**Validation logic** (pattern → CEL expression)

input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: unique-ingress-host
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Unique Ingress Host
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Ingress
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      An Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally. In most cases,
      these hosts should be unique across the cluster to ensure no routing conflicts occur.
      This policy checks an incoming Ingress resource to ensure its hosts are unique to the cluster.
      It also ensures that only a single host may be specified in a given manifest.      
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: false
  rules:
    - name: check-single-host-create
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Ingress
      context:
        - name: hosts
          apiCall:
            urlPath: "/apis/networking.k8s.io/v1/ingresses"
            jmesPath: "items[].spec.rules[].host"
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: Equals
          value: CREATE
      validate:
        message: "The Ingress host name must be unique."
        deny:
          conditions:
            all:
              - key: "{{ request.object.spec.rules[].host }}"
                operator: AnyIn
                value: "{{ hosts }}"
    - name: check-single-host-update
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Ingress
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: Equals
          value: UPDATE
      context:
        - name: allhosts
          apiCall:
            urlPath: "/apis/networking.k8s.io/v1/ingresses"
            jmesPath: "items[?metadata.uid!='{{ request.object.metadata.uid }}'].spec.rules[].host"
      validate:
        message: "The Ingress host name must be unique."
        deny:
          conditions:
            all:
              - key: "{{ request.object.spec.rules[].host }}"
                operator: AnyIn
                value: "{{ allhosts }}"
    - name: deny-multiple-hosts
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Ingress
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: AnyIn
          value:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        - key: "{{ request.object.spec.rules[].host | length(@)}}"
          operator: GreaterThan
          value: 1
      validate:
        message: "An Ingress resource may only contain a single host entry."
        deny: {}
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      An Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally. In most cases,
      these hosts should be unique across the cluster to ensure no routing conflicts occur.
      This policy checks an incoming Ingress resource to ensure its hosts are unique to the cluster.
      It also ensures that only a single host may be specified in a given manifest.
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Ingress
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Unique Ingress Host
  name: unique-ingress-host
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - networking.k8s.io
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - ingresses
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  variables:
    - name: allIngresses
      apiCall:
        urlPath: "/apis/networking.k8s.io/v1/ingresses"
        jmesPath: "items"
    - name: otherIngresses
      expression: >-
        variables.allIngresses.filter(i, i.metadata.uid != object.metadata.uid)
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        !object.spec.rules.exists(newRule,
          variables.otherIngresses.exists(ing,
            has(ing.spec.rules) &&
            ing.spec.rules.exists(existingRule,
              has(existingRule.host) && has(newRule.host) &&
              existingRule.host == newRule.host)))
      message: "The Ingress host name must be unique."
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.rules.size() <= 1
      message: "An Ingress resource may only contain a single host entry."

| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` (with explicit `apiGroups`, `apiVersions`, `operations`) |
| `rules[].context[].apiCall` | `spec.variables[].apiCall` |
| `rules[].preconditions` (operation filter) | Eliminated — the `otherIngresses` variable handles both operations: on CREATE `object.metadata.uid` is empty so nothing is excluded; on UPDATE the current object is excluded by uid |
| `rules[].validate.deny.conditions` (3 rules) | `spec.validations` (2 CEL expressions) |
| `policies.kyverno.io/minversion` annotation | Dropped (not carried over in reference examples) |

The three original rules collapsed into two `validations`:
1. **Uniqueness** — uses nested `exists()` to check whether any rule host in the new object matches a host in any other ingress, without needing `flatten()`
2. **Single host** — simple `object.spec.rules.size() <= 1`

description: a Kubernetes Service of type NodePort uses a host port to receive traffic from any source
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_restrict_nodeport.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy a Kubernetes Service of type NodePort uses a host port to receive traffic from any source. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_restrict_nodeport.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-nodeport
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow NodePort
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Service
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A Kubernetes Service of type NodePort uses a host port to receive traffic from
      any source. A NetworkPolicy cannot be used to control traffic to host ports.
      Although NodePort Services can be useful, their use must be limited to Services
      with additional upstream security checks. This policy validates that any new Services
      do not use the `NodePort` type.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-nodeport
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Service
    validate:
      message: "Services of type NodePort are not allowed."
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(type): "!NodePort"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A Kubernetes Service of type NodePort uses a host port to receive traffic from
      any source. A NetworkPolicy cannot be used to control traffic to host ports.
      Although NodePort Services can be useful, their use must be limited to Services
      with additional upstream security checks. This policy validates that any new Services
      do not use the `NodePort` type.
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Service
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow NodePort
  name: restrict-nodeport
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ''
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - services
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        !has(object.spec.type) || object.spec.type != 'NodePort'
      messageExpression: >-
        'Service ' + object.metadata.name + ' is of type NodePort which is not allowed.'

The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key changes made:

**`apiVersion` / `kind`**: `kyverno.io/v1` / `ClusterPolicy` → `policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `ValidatingPolicy`

**`spec.validationFailureAction: Audit`** → `spec.validationActions: [Audit]`

**`spec.background: true`** → `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true`

**`spec.rules[].match`** → `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` with explicit `apiGroups`, `apiVersions`, `operations` (CREATE/UPDATE), and plural lowercase `resources: [services]`

**`spec.rules[].validate.pattern`** → `spec.validations[].expression` as a CEL expression. The old pattern `=(type): "!NodePort"` (optional field must not equal NodePort) becomes `!has(object.spec.type) || object.spec.type != 'NodePort'`, which handles both the absent-field case and the explicit non-NodePort case.

**`validate.message`** → `messageExpression` using CEL string interpolation to include the resource name in the error message.

description: mutates Pods and Services to add the label 'foo: bar' to metadata.labels. The label must be added if absent and left unchanged if already present.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that mutates Pods and Services to add the label 'foo: bar' to metadata.labels. The label must be added if absent and left unchanged if already present. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/gen_add_default_labels.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-foo-bar-label
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods", "services"]
  matchConditions:
    - name: label-foo-not-present
      expression: |
        !has(object.metadata.labels) ||
        !('foo' in object.metadata.labels)
  mutations:
    - patchType: ApplyConfiguration
      applyConfiguration:
        expression: |
          Object{
            metadata: Object.metadata{
              labels: {
                "foo": "bar"
              }
            }
          }
raw_log:
The policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's what it does and why each part is structured this way:

**`matchConstraints.resourceRules`** — targets both `pods` and `services` in the core API group (`""`), for `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations. This replaces the old `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources` structure.

**`matchConditions`** — the CEL expression `!has(object.metadata.labels) || !('foo' in object.metadata.labels)` ensures the mutation only fires when the label is absent. If `foo: bar` is already present, the condition is false and no mutation runs — satisfying the "leave unchanged if already present" requirement.

**`mutations[].patchType: ApplyConfiguration`** — uses a CEL `Object{}` literal to describe the desired state. The `ApplyConfiguration` patch type merges rather than replaces, so any existing labels on the resource are preserved and only `foo: bar` is added.

**`apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1`** — used as requested (stable v1 API, consistent with the `ValidatingPolicy` reference example which also uses `v1`).

description: generates a NetworkPolicy named 'default-deny' in any newly-created Namespace. The generated NetworkPolicy must select all Pods (empty podSelector), include both 'Ingress' and 'Egress' policyTypes, and define no ingress or egress rules so all traffic is denied by default.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ GeneratingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that generates a NetworkPolicy named 'default-deny' in any newly-created Namespace. The generated NetworkPolicy must select all Pods (empty podSelector), include both 'Ingress' and 'Egress' policyTypes, and define no ingress or egress rules so all traffic is denied by default. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/gen_create_networkpolicy.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  name: default-deny-networkpolicy
spec:
  evaluation:
    synchronize:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE"]
        resources: ["namespaces"]
  variables:
    - name: targetNs
      expression: "object.metadata.name"
    - name: downstream
      expression: >-
        [
          {
            "kind": dyn("NetworkPolicy"),
            "apiVersion": dyn("networking.k8s.io/v1"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name": "default-deny"
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "podSelector": dyn({}),
              "policyTypes": dyn(["Ingress", "Egress"])
            })
          }
        ]
  generate:
    - expression: generator.Apply(variables.targetNs, variables.downstream)
raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what was done and why:

**Structure decisions, guided by the reference examples:**

| Field | Value | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion` | `policies.kyverno.io/v1` | Task requirement; matches the `ValidatingPolicy` reference |
| `kind` | `GeneratingPolicy` | New 1.16+ kind replacing `ClusterPolicy` generate rules |
| `matchConstraints.resourceRules` | `namespaces`, `CREATE` only | Triggers only on new Namespace creation |
| `evaluation.synchronize.enabled` | `true` | Keeps generated resources in sync if the policy changes |
| `variables.targetNs` | `object.metadata.name` | Extracts the new namespace name to target the generated resource |
| `variables.downstream` | CEL list with `dyn()` casts | Required pattern from the reference for typed/untyped field mixing |
| `generate` | `generator.Apply(...)` | Standard generator call matching the reference |

**Generated NetworkPolicy characteristics:**
- `podSelector: {}` — selects all Pods in the namespace
- `policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]` — both directions declared
- No `ingress` or `egress` rule lists — omitting them with the types declared causes Kubernetes to deny all matching traffic by default

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers add any Linux capability via securityContext.capabilities.add beyond the Pod Security Standards baseline allow-list (AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT).
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers add any Linux capability via securityContext.capabilities.add beyond the Pod Security Standards baseline allow-list (AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT). Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/gen_disallow_capabilities.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-capabilities-baseline
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Linux Capabilities to PSS Baseline Allowlist
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Deployment, CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs whose containers, initContainers,
      or ephemeralContainers add any Linux capability via
      securityContext.capabilities.add that is not in the Pod Security Standards
      baseline allowlist. Permitted capabilities: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN,
      DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP,
      SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT.
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ''
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs
  variables:
    - name: allowedCaps
      expression: >-
        ["AUDIT_WRITE", "CHOWN", "DAC_OVERRIDE", "FOWNER", "FSETID", "KILL",
         "MKNOD", "NET_BIND_SERVICE", "SETFCAP", "SETGID", "SETPCAP", "SETUID",
         "SYS_CHROOT"]
    - name: podSpec
      expression: >-
        object.kind == "Pod" ? object.spec :
        object.kind == "Deployment" ? object.spec.template.spec :
        object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.podSpec.containers.all(c,
          !has(c.securityContext) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.capabilities) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) ||
          c.securityContext.capabilities.add.all(cap, cap in variables.allowedCaps)
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        object.kind + ' ' + object.metadata.name +
        ' has containers that add Linux capabilities beyond the PSS baseline allowlist.' +
        ' Offending containers: [' +
        variables.podSpec.containers.filter(c,
          has(c.securityContext) &&
          has(c.securityContext.capabilities) &&
          has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) &&
          c.securityContext.capabilities.add.exists(cap, !(cap in variables.allowedCaps))
        ).map(c, c.name).join(', ') +
        ']. Permitted: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL,' +
        ' MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT.'
    - expression: >-
        !has(variables.podSpec.initContainers) ||
        variables.podSpec.initContainers.all(c,
          !has(c.securityContext) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.capabilities) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) ||
          c.securityContext.capabilities.add.all(cap, cap in variables.allowedCaps)
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        object.kind + ' ' + object.metadata.name +
        ' has initContainers that add Linux capabilities beyond the PSS baseline allowlist.' +
        ' Offending initContainers: [' +
        variables.podSpec.initContainers.filter(c,
          has(c.securityContext) &&
          has(c.securityContext.capabilities) &&
          has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) &&
          c.securityContext.capabilities.add.exists(cap, !(cap in variables.allowedCaps))
        ).map(c, c.name).join(', ') +
        ']. Permitted: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL,' +
        ' MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT.'
    - expression: >-
        !has(variables.podSpec.ephemeralContainers) ||
        variables.podSpec.ephemeralContainers.all(c,
          !has(c.securityContext) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.capabilities) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) ||
          c.securityContext.capabilities.add.all(cap, cap in variables.allowedCaps)
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        object.kind + ' ' + object.metadata.name +
        ' has ephemeralContainers that add Linux capabilities beyond the PSS baseline allowlist.' +
        ' Offending ephemeralContainers: [' +
        variables.podSpec.ephemeralContainers.filter(c,
          has(c.securityContext) &&
          has(c.securityContext.capabilities) &&
          has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) &&
          c.securityContext.capabilities.add.exists(cap, !(cap in variables.allowedCaps))
        ).map(c, c.name).join(', ') +
        ']. Permitted: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL,' +
        ' MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT.'
raw_log:
Written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a breakdown of the design:

---

## Structure

**`apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy`** — matches the reference pattern for Kyverno 1.16+.

**`spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true`** — enables background scanning of existing resources (mirrors old `spec.background: true`).

**`spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules`** — three entries covering:
- `""` / `v1` / `pods`
- `apps` / `v1` / `deployments`
- `batch` / `v1` / `cronjobs`

All on `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations.

---

## CEL Variables (`spec.variables`)

Two shared variables eliminate repetition across the three validations:

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `allowedCaps` | CEL list of the 13 PSS baseline capabilities |
| `podSpec` | Ternary that resolves to the pod spec for any of the three resource kinds (`object.spec` → `object.spec.template.spec` → `object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec`) |

---

## Three Validations (`spec.validations`)

One per container list type, each with `validationActions: [Deny]`:

1. **`containers`** — accessed directly (required field, no `has()` guard needed)
2. **`initContainers`** — guarded with `!has(...) ||` (optional field)
3. **`ephemeralContainers`** — guarded with `!has(...) ||` (optional field)

Each validation checks that every capability in `capabilities.add` is a member of `variables.allowedCaps`. The `messageExpression` uses `.filter().map(c, c.name).join(', ')` to name the offending containers in the denial message.

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that set spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, or spec.hostIPC to true. All three host namespace fields must be checked.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that set spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, or spec.hostIPC to true. All three host namespace fields must be checked. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/gen_disallow_host_namespaces.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-host-namespaces
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Host Namespaces
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Deployment, CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Sharing the host namespaces (hostNetwork, hostPID, hostIPC) between a
      container and the node allows the container to interact with the node's
      network stack, process list, and IPC resources directly, which is a
      significant security risk. This policy denies any Pod, Deployment, or
      CronJob that sets hostNetwork, hostPID, or hostIPC to true.
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ''
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        !(request.kind.kind == 'Pod' && (
          (has(object.spec.hostNetwork) && object.spec.hostNetwork) ||
          (has(object.spec.hostPID) && object.spec.hostPID) ||
          (has(object.spec.hostIPC) && object.spec.hostIPC)
        ))
      message: >-
        Pods must not set spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, or spec.hostIPC to true.
    - expression: >-
        !(request.kind.kind == 'Deployment' && (
          (has(object.spec.template.spec.hostNetwork) && object.spec.template.spec.hostNetwork) ||
          (has(object.spec.template.spec.hostPID) && object.spec.template.spec.hostPID) ||
          (has(object.spec.template.spec.hostIPC) && object.spec.template.spec.hostIPC)
        ))
      message: >-
        Deployments must not set spec.template.spec.hostNetwork,
        spec.template.spec.hostPID, or spec.template.spec.hostIPC to true.
    - expression: >-
        !(request.kind.kind == 'CronJob' && (
          (has(object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.hostNetwork) && object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.hostNetwork) ||
          (has(object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.hostPID) && object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.hostPID) ||
          (has(object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.hostIPC) && object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.hostIPC)
        ))
      message: >-
        CronJobs must not set hostNetwork, hostPID, or hostIPC to true
        in spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.
raw_log:
Written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the design decisions:

**Structure** follows the reference `after.yaml` pattern:
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1`, `kind: ValidatingPolicy`
- `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` with one entry per resource group (Pods in `""`, Deployments in `apps`, CronJobs in `batch`)
- `spec.validationActions: [Deny]` since the requirement is to deny
- `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true` for background scanning

**Three separate `validations` entries**, one per resource kind, each using `request.kind.kind` to guard the check:
- **Pod** — checks `object.spec.{hostNetwork,hostPID,hostIPC}`
- **Deployment** — checks `object.spec.template.spec.{...}`
- **CronJob** — checks `object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.{...}` (the extra nesting through `jobTemplate`)

**CEL pattern**: each expression returns `false` (admission denied) when the kind matches *and* any of the three fields is `true`. `has()` guards each field access so the expression is safe when the field is absent (defaults to `false`/unset, which is allowed).

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that use any hostPath volume. The check must reject any volume in spec.volumes whose hostPath field is set, regardless of the hostPath value.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that use any hostPath volume. The check must reject any volume in spec.volumes whose hostPath field is set, regardless of the hostPath value. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/gen_disallow_host_path.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: deny-hostpath-volumes
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Deny hostPath Volumes
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      hostPath volumes grant Pods direct access to the host filesystem, which
      can be exploited to escape container isolation or read sensitive host data.
      This policy denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that define any volume
      with the hostPath field set, regardless of the path value.
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ''
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.kind == 'Pod' ?
          (!has(object.spec.volumes) || !object.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.hostPath))) :
        object.kind == 'Deployment' ?
          (!has(object.spec.template.spec.volumes) || !object.spec.template.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.hostPath))) :
          (!has(object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes) || !object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.hostPath)))
      messageExpression: >-
        object.kind + ' ' + object.metadata.name + ' defines a hostPath volume, which is not permitted.'
raw_log:
The policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the design decisions:

**Structure** (matching the reference `after.yaml` pattern):
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ValidatingPolicy`
- `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true` for background scanning
- `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with three entries — one per resource type (Pods in `""`, Deployments in `apps`, CronJobs in `batch`)
- `spec.validationActions: [Deny]` to hard-reject

**CEL expression** — a ternary cascade on `object.kind` to handle the three different volume paths:

| Kind | Volume path |
|------|-------------|
| `Pod` | `spec.volumes` |
| `Deployment` | `spec.template.spec.volumes` |
| `CronJob` | `spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes` |

For each branch the check is: "volumes list is absent **or** no volume in it has `hostPath` set." The `has(v.hostPath)` predicate catches any hostPath entry regardless of its value (empty string, `/`, etc.), satisfying the "regardless of the hostPath value" requirement. CEL short-circuits on the `object.kind` test, so accessing deep CronJob paths when evaluating a Pod never causes a field-not-found error.

description: denies any Pod, Deployment, or CronJob whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers set securityContext.privileged to true.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies any Pod, Deployment, or CronJob whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers set securityContext.privileged to true. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/gen_disallow_privileged.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: deny-privileged-containers
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Deny Privileged Containers
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Deployment, CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Privileged containers share namespaces with the underlying host and have
      nearly unrestricted access to host resources. This policy denies any Pod,
      Deployment, or CronJob whose containers, initContainers, or
      ephemeralContainers set securityContext.privileged to true.
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ''
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  variables:
    - name: podSpec
      expression: >-
        object.kind == 'Pod' ? object.spec :
        object.kind == 'Deployment' ? object.spec.template.spec :
        object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        (!has(variables.podSpec.containers) ||
          !variables.podSpec.containers.exists(c,
            has(c.securityContext) &&
            has(c.securityContext.privileged) &&
            c.securityContext.privileged == true)) &&
        (!has(variables.podSpec.initContainers) ||
          !variables.podSpec.initContainers.exists(c,
            has(c.securityContext) &&
            has(c.securityContext.privileged) &&
            c.securityContext.privileged == true)) &&
        (!has(variables.podSpec.ephemeralContainers) ||
          !variables.podSpec.ephemeralContainers.exists(c,
            has(c.securityContext) &&
            has(c.securityContext.privileged) &&
            c.securityContext.privileged == true))
      messageExpression: >-
        object.kind + ' ' + object.metadata.name +
        ' is not allowed to run privileged containers. Remove or set to false ' +
        'securityContext.privileged in all containers, initContainers, and ephemeralContainers.'
raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the design decisions:

**Structure** (matching the `validating-policy` reference):
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy`
- `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true` for background scanning
- `spec.validationActions: [Deny]` to hard-block violations

**`matchConstraints.resourceRules`** — three separate entries:
| Resource | apiGroup | resource |
|---|---|---|
| Pod | `""` (core) | `pods` |
| Deployment | `apps` | `deployments` |
| CronJob | `batch` | `cronjobs` |
Both `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations are covered.

**`variables`** — a single `podSpec` variable uses a CEL ternary chain to resolve the correct pod spec path for each kind:
- Pod → `object.spec`
- Deployment → `object.spec.template.spec`
- CronJob → `object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec`

**`validations`** — the CEL expression checks all three container list fields (`containers`, `initContainers`, `ephemeralContainers`) against `variables.podSpec`, guarding each with `has()` before traversing into optional fields. A descriptive `messageExpression` names the offending resource in the denial message.

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Enforce the presence of required labels that identify semantic attributes
      of applications or Deployments. Specifically, ensure that the standard
      label `app.kubernetes.io/name` is present on Pods with a non-empty value

**Structure** (from reference pattern):
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — new 1.16+ API
- `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` — replaces the old `spec.rules[].match` block; targets `pods` in core group (`''`) for `CREATE` and `UPDATE`
- `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true` — replaces `spec.background: true`
- `spec.validations[].expression` — CEL expression replaces the old `validate.pattern`

**Task-specific choices**:
- `validationActions: [Deny]` — the task requires blocking admission (the reference used `Audit` since the original ClusterPolicy did; here the requirement is explicitly to deny)

description: requires every container in a Pod to set spec.containers[].resources.requests.memory, spec.containers[].resources.requests.cpu, and spec.containers[].resources.limits.memory. Deny admission if any container is missing any of these three fields. CPU limit is NOT required.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that requires every container in a Pod to set spec.containers[].resources.requests.memory, spec.containers[].resources.requests.cpu, and spec.containers[].resources.limits.memory. Deny admission if any container is missing any of these three fields. CPU limit is NOT required. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/gen_require_resource_limits.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Requires every container in a Pod to specify resource requests for CPU
      and memory, and a resource limit for memory. Admission is denied if any
      container is missing resources.requests.cpu, resources.requests.memory,
      or resources.limits.memory. A CPU limit is not required.
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Container Resources
  name: require-container-resources
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ''
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(c,
          has(c.resources) &&
          has(c.resources.requests) &&
          has(c.resources.requests.cpu) &&
          has(c.resources.requests.memory) &&
          has(c.resources.limits) &&
          has(c.resources.limits.memory)
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        'Pod ' + object.metadata.name + ': all containers must set
        resources.requests.cpu, resources.requests.memory, and
        resources.limits.memory. Non-compliant containers: ' +
        object.spec.containers.filter(c,
          !has(c.resources) ||
          !has(c.resources.requests) ||
          !has(c.resources.requests.cpu) ||
          !has(c.resources.requests.memory) ||
          !has(c.resources.limits) ||
          !has(c.resources.limits.memory)
        ).map(c, c.name).join(', ')
raw_log:
Written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's what the policy does and why each choice was made:

**Structure** — follows the `policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `ValidatingPolicy` schema seen in the reference `after.yaml`, with `matchConstraints`, `validationActions`, and `validations` (CEL-based).

**`validationActions: [Deny]`** — blocks admission immediately (vs `Audit` which only logs). The task requires denying admission.

**`evaluation.background.enabled: true`** — allows the policy to also scan already-running Pods, consistent with the reference pattern.

**CEL expression** — uses `.all()` to assert that every container satisfies all three conditions:
- `has(c.resources.requests.cpu)` — requests.cpu present
- `has(c.resources.requests.memory)` — requests.memory present
- `has(c.resources.limits.memory)` — limits.memory present
- `has(c.resources)` and `has(c.resources.requests)` / `has(c.resources.limits)` guard against the parent fields being absent entirely (CEL will error without these guards)

CPU limit is intentionally absent from the checks.

**`messageExpression`** — uses `.filter()` + `.map()` + `.join()` to name the specific non-compliant containers in the denial message, making it actionable for the submitter.

description: only allows Pod container images that begin with the prefix 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'. Deny any Pod whose containers use images from any other registry.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that only allows Pod container images that begin with the prefix 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'. Deny any Pod whose containers use images from any other registry. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/gen_restrict_registries.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-image-registries
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Image Registries
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Restricts Pod container images to only those sourced from approved
      registries. All containers (including init and ephemeral containers)
      must use images prefixed with 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'. Images from
      any other registry are denied.
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ''
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(c,
          c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') || c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')) &&
        (!has(object.spec.initContainers) || object.spec.initContainers.all(c,
          c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') || c.image.startsWith('bar.io/'))) &&
        (!has(object.spec.ephemeralContainers) || object.spec.ephemeralContainers.all(c,
          c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') || c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')))
      messageExpression: >-
        'Pod ' + object.metadata.name + ' contains images from disallowed registries. ' +
        'Allowed prefixes are eu.foo.io/ and bar.io/. Offending images: ' +
        (object.spec.containers.filter(c,
          !c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') && !c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')
        ).map(c, c.image) +
        (has(object.spec.initContainers) ? object.spec.initContainers.filter(c,
          !c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') && !c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')
        ).map(c, c.image) : []) +
        (has(object.spec.ephemeralContainers) ? object.spec.ephemeralContainers.filter(c,
          !c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') && !c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')
        ).map(c, c.image) : [])).join(', ')
raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key design decisions based on studying the reference examples:

**Structure follows `policies.kyverno.io/v1` ValidatingPolicy conventions:**

- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the new API group and kind
- `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` — replaces the old `spec.rules[].match` block; targets `pods` in core API group (`''`), both CREATE and UPDATE operations
- `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true` — enables background scanning of existing resources
- `spec.validationActions: [Deny]` — hard deny (vs `Audit` which only logs)
- `spec.validations[].expression` — CEL expression replacing the old `validate.pattern`

**The CEL validation expression covers all three container types:**

1. `spec.containers` — always present, no guard needed
2. `spec.initContainers` — guarded with `!has(...)` since it's optional
3. `spec.ephemeralContainers` — guarded with `!has(...)` since it's optional

Each container's `image` must `startsWith('eu.foo.io/')` or `startsWith('bar.io/')`.

**`messageExpression`** dynamically collects and lists all offending image names across all container types, giving operators a clear denial reason.

apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-labels
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Labels
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Label
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Labels are used as an important source of metadata describing objects in various ways
      or triggering other functionality. Labels are also a very basic concept and should be
      used throughout Kubernetes. This policy performs a simple mutation which adds a label
      `foo=bar` to Pods, Services, ConfigMaps, and Secrets.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: add-labels
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
          - Service
          - ConfigMap
          - Secret
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        metadata:
          labels:
            foo: bar

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-labels
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Labels
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Label

      Labels are used as an important source of metadata describing objects in various ways
      or triggering other functionality. Labels are also a very basic concept and should be
      used throughout Kubernetes. This policy performs a simple mutation which adds a label
      `foo=bar` to Pods, Services, ConfigMaps, and Secrets.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods", "services", "configmaps", "secrets"]
  mutations:
    - patchType: ApplyConfiguration
      applyConfiguration:
        expression: |
          Object{
            metadata: Object.metadata{
              labels: {
                "foo": "bar"
              }
            }
          }

description: pods which don't specify at least resource requests are assigned a QoS class of BestEffort which can hog resources for other Pods on Nodes
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_default_resources.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy pods which don't specify at least resource requests are assigned a QoS class of BestEffort which can hog resources for other Pods on Nodes. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/cp_add_default_resources.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
input_snippet:
apiVersion : kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-default-resources
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Default Resources
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.10.0-alpha.2
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.7.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.26"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Pods which don't specify at least resource requests are assigned a QoS class
      of BestEffort which can hog resources for other Pods on Nodes. At a minimum,
      all Pods should specify resource requests in order to be labeled as the QoS
      class Burstable. This sample mutates any container in a Pod which doesn't
      specify memory or cpu requests to apply some sane defaults.
spec:
  background: false
  rules:
  - name: add-default-requests
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    preconditions:
      any:
      - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
        operator: AnyIn
        value:
        - CREATE
        - UPDATE
    mutate:
      foreach:
      - list: "request.object.spec.[ephemeralContainers, initContainers, containers][]"
        patchStrategicMerge:
          spec:
            containers:
            - (name): "{{element.name}}"
              resources:
                requests:
                  +(memory): "100Mi"
                  +(cpu): "100m"
raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key changes made:

**API/Kind changes:**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1`
- `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `kind: MutatingPolicy`

**Spec restructure:**
- `spec.rules[].match` → `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with explicit `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: ["v1"]`, and `resources: ["pods"]`
- The `preconditions` (CREATE/UPDATE operation filter) is now expressed directly in `matchConstraints.resourceRules[].operations`
- A `matchConditions` CEL expression replaces the old `preconditions` block, filtering only pods that actually have at least one container missing cpu or memory requests (preserving the `+()` "only if absent" semantics)

**Mutation logic:**
- `spec.rules[].mutate.foreach` with `patchStrategicMerge` → `spec.mutations[].patchType: ApplyConfiguration` with a CEL `applyConfiguration.expression`
- The `foreach` over all container lists is replaced by `.map()` calls in CEL over `containers`, `initContainers`, and `ephemeralContainers`
- The `+(memory)` / `+(cpu)` "add if absent" semantics are replicated with CEL ternary: `has(c.resources.requests.cpu) ? existing_value : "100m"`
- `background: false` is dropped (the new API defaults background to disabled for MutatingPolicy)
- Kyverno-version/minversion annotations are removed as they no longer apply to the new API

kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-ndots
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add ndots

    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod

        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]

      applyConfiguration:
        expression: |
          Object{
            spec: Object.spec{
              dnsConfig: Object.spec.dnsConfig{

**`apiVersion` / `kind`**: Changed from `kyverno.io/v1` / `ClusterPolicy` to `policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `MutatingPolicy`.

**`spec.rules` → flattened spec**: The new format replaces the `rules` array with:
- `matchConstraints.resourceRules` — replaces `match.any[].resources`, specifying the Pod resource (`""` core API group, `v1`, `CREATE`/`UPDATE` operations, `pods` resource).
- `mutations` — replaces `mutate`, using `patchType: ApplyConfiguration` with a CEL `expression` that builds the desired object shape, rather than `patchStrategicMerge`. The expression sets `spec.dnsConfig.options` with the `ndots: "1"` entry.

No `matchConditions` were needed since the original policy applies unconditionally to all Pods (no precondition filtering).

description: to better control the number of resources that can be created in a given Namespace and provide default resource consumption limits for Pods, ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources are recommended
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_ns_quota.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ GeneratingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy to better control the number of resources that can be created in a given Namespace and provide default resource consumption limits for Pods, ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources are recommended. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/cp_add_ns_quota.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-ns-quota
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Quota
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Multi-Tenancy, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: ResourceQuota, LimitRange
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      To better control the number of resources that can be created in a given
      Namespace and provide default resource consumption limits for Pods,
      ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources are recommended.
      This policy will generate ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources when
      a new Namespace is created.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: generate-resourcequota
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Namespace
    generate:
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: ResourceQuota
      name: default-resourcequota
      synchronize: true
      namespace: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
      data:
        spec:
          hard:
            requests.cpu: '4'
            requests.memory: '16Gi'
            limits.cpu: '4'
            limits.memory: '16Gi'
  - name: generate-limitrange
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Namespace
    generate:
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: LimitRange
      name: default-limitrange
      synchronize: true
      namespace: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
      data:
        spec:
          limits:
          - default:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 1Gi
            defaultRequest:
              cpu: 200m
              memory: 256Mi
            type: Container
raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key changes made:

**`apiVersion` / `kind`**
- `kyverno.io/v1` / `ClusterPolicy` → `policies.kyverno.io/v1alpha1` / `GeneratingPolicy`

**`minversion` annotation**
- `1.6.0` → `1.15.0`

**`spec` restructured entirely:**
- The two separate `rules` (each with `match` + `generate`) are replaced by a flat structure:
  - `spec.evaluation.synchronize.enabled: true` — replaces the per-rule `synchronize: true`
  - `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` — replaces the per-rule `match.any[].resources.kinds: [Namespace]`, using CEL-style resource rules with `apiGroups`, `apiVersions`, `operations`, and `resources`
  - `spec.variables` — CEL variables: `targetNs` (from `object.metadata.name`) and `downstream` (a CEL array expression describing both the `ResourceQuota` and `LimitRange` objects to generate, using `dyn()` for dynamic typing)
  - `spec.generate` — a single `generator.Apply(variables.targetNs, variables.downstream)` expression that applies all downstream resources to the target namespace

description: the Kubernetes cluster autoscaler does not evict pods that  use hostPath or emptyDir volumes
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_safe_to_evict.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy the Kubernetes cluster autoscaler does not evict pods that  use hostPath or emptyDir volumes. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/cp_add_safe_to_evict.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-safe-to-evict
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Safe To Evict
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Annotation
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The Kubernetes cluster autoscaler does not evict pods that 
      use hostPath or emptyDir volumes. To allow eviction of these pods, the annotation 
      cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict=true must be added to the pods. 
spec: 
  rules: 
  - name: annotate-empty-dir
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        metadata:
          annotations:
            +(cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict): "true"
        spec:          
          volumes: 
          - <(emptyDir): {}
  - name: annotate-host-path
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        metadata:
          annotations:
            +(cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict): "true"
        spec:          
          volumes: 
          - hostPath:
              <(path): "*"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-safe-to-evict
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Safe To Evict
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Annotation
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The Kubernetes cluster autoscaler does not evict pods that 
      use hostPath or emptyDir volumes. To allow eviction of these pods, the annotation 
      cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict=true must be added to the pods.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
  matchConditions:
    - name: has-emptydir-or-hostpath
      expression: |
        has(object.spec.volumes) && 
        object.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.emptyDir) || has(v.hostPath))
    - name: annotation-not-present
      expression: |
        !has(object.metadata.annotations) || 
        !object.metadata.annotations.exists(k, k == 'cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict')
  mutations:
    - patchType: ApplyConfiguration
      applyConfiguration:
        expression: |
          Object{
            metadata: Object.metadata{
              annotations: {
                "cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict": "true"
              }
            }
          }
raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key changes made:

- **`apiVersion`**: `kyverno.io/v1` → `policies.kyverno.io/v1`
- **`kind`**: `ClusterPolicy` → `MutatingPolicy`
- **`spec.rules`** replaced with the new structure:
  - **`spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules`**: replaces the `match.any[].resources.kinds` block — targets Pods (`""` apiGroup, `v1`, `CREATE`/`UPDATE`)
  - **`spec.matchConditions`**: replaces the `patchStrategicMerge` anchor-based conditions (`<(emptyDir)`, `<(path)`) with CEL expressions that check for the presence of `emptyDir` or `hostPath` volumes, and that the annotation isn't already set (consolidating both old rules)
  - **`spec.mutations`**: replaces `mutate.patchStrategicMerge` with a `patchType: ApplyConfiguration` mutation using a CEL `Object{}` expression to add the `cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict: "true"` annotation

description: a Pod securityContext entry defines fields such as the user and group which should be used to run the Pod
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_securitycontext.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy a Pod securityContext entry defines fields such as the user and group which should be used to run the Pod. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/cp_add_securitycontext.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-default-securitycontext
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Default securityContext
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A Pod securityContext entry defines fields such as the user and group which should be used to run the Pod.
      Sometimes choosing default values for users rather than blocking is a better alternative to not impede
      such Pod definitions. This policy will mutate a Pod to set `runAsNonRoot`, `runAsUser`, `runAsGroup`, and 
      `fsGroup` fields within the Pod securityContext if they are not already set.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: add-default-securitycontext
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        spec:
          securityContext:
            +(runAsNonRoot): true
            +(runAsUser): 1000
            +(runAsGroup): 3000
            +(fsGroup): 2000
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-default-securitycontext
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Default securityContext
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A Pod securityContext entry defines fields such as the user and group which should be used to run the Pod.
      Sometimes choosing default values for users rather than blocking is a better alternative to not impede
      such Pod definitions. This policy will mutate a Pod to set `runAsNonRoot`, `runAsUser`, `runAsGroup`, and
      `fsGroup` fields within the Pod securityContext if they are not already set.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
  matchConditions:
    - name: missing-any-securitycontext-field
      expression: |
        !has(object.spec.securityContext) ||
        !has(object.spec.securityContext.runAsNonRoot) ||
        !has(object.spec.securityContext.runAsUser) ||
        !has(object.spec.securityContext.runAsGroup) ||
        !has(object.spec.securityContext.fsGroup)
  mutations:
    - patchType: ApplyConfiguration
      applyConfiguration:
        expression: |
          Object{
            spec: Object.spec{
              securityContext: Object.spec.securityContext{
                runAsNonRoot: !has(object.spec.securityContext) || !has(object.spec.securityContext.runAsNonRoot)
                  ? true
                  : object.spec.securityContext.runAsNonRoot,
                runAsUser: !has(object.spec.securityContext) || !has(object.spec.securityContext.runAsUser)
                  ? 1000
                  : object.spec.securityContext.runAsUser,
                runAsGroup: !has(object.spec.securityContext) || !has(object.spec.securityContext.runAsGroup)
                  ? 3000
                  : object.spec.securityContext.runAsGroup,
                fsGroup: !has(object.spec.securityContext) || !has(object.spec.securityContext.fsGroup)
                  ? 2000
                  : object.spec.securityContext.fsGroup
              }
            }
          }
raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key changes made:

**Structural changes:**
- `apiVersion`: `kyverno.io/v1` → `policies.kyverno.io/v1`
- `kind`: `ClusterPolicy` → `MutatingPolicy`
- `policies.kyverno.io/minversion`: updated to `1.16.0`

**Spec restructuring:**
- `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Pod]` → `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with explicit `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: ["v1"]`, `operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]`, `resources: ["pods"]`

**Mutation logic:**
- `patchStrategicMerge` with `+(field)` (if-not-present) anchors → `mutations` array using `patchType: ApplyConfiguration` with a CEL expression
- Each `+(field): value` becomes a conditional in the CEL `applyConfiguration` expression: if the field is absent, set the default; otherwise preserve the existing value
- A `matchConditions` entry is added to guard the mutation — only fires when at least one of the four fields is missing, avoiding unnecessary patches

description: pod tolerations are used to schedule on Nodes which have a matching taint
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_tolerations.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy pod tolerations are used to schedule on Nodes which have a matching taint. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/cp_add_tolerations.yaml.

Reference conversion examples are in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/reference — each subdirectory has a before.yaml (old ClusterPolicy) and after.yaml (converted policy). Study these examples before converting.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-tolerations
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Tolerations
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.7.1
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >- 
      Pod tolerations are used to schedule on Nodes which have
      a matching taint. This policy adds the toleration `org.com/role=service:NoSchedule`
      if existing tolerations do not contain the key `org.com/role`.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: service-toleration
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    preconditions:
      any:
      - key: "org.com/role"
        operator: AnyNotIn
        value: "{{ request.object.spec.tolerations[].key || `[]` }}"
    mutate:
      patchesJson6902: |-
        - op: add
          path: "/spec/tolerations/-"
          value:
            key: org.com/role
            operator: Equal
            value: service
            effect: NoSchedule
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-tolerations
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Tolerations
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Pod tolerations are used to schedule on Nodes which have
      a matching taint. This policy adds the toleration `org.com/role=service:NoSchedule`
      if existing tolerations do not contain the key `org.com/role`.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
  matchConditions:
    - name: missing-role-toleration
      expression: |
        !has(object.spec.tolerations) ||
        !object.spec.tolerations.exists(t, t.key == 'org.com/role')
  mutations:
    - patchType: JSONPatch
      jsonPatch:
        expression: |
          [
            JSONPatch{
              op: "add",
              path: "/spec/tolerations/-",
              value: Object{
                key: "org.com/role",
                operator: "Equal",
                value: "service",
                effect: "NoSchedule"
              }
            }
          ]

The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key transformations applied:

**API/Kind:**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: MutatingPolicy`

**Metadata:**
- Dropped `kyverno.io/kyverno-version` and `kyverno.io/kubernetes-version` (engine-internal, not relevant in the new API)
- Updated `policies.kyverno.io/minversion` to `1.16.0`

**`spec.rules[].match`** → **`spec.matchConstraints`:**
- `resources.kinds: [Pod]` → `resourceRules` with explicit `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: ["v1"]`, `operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]`, `resources: ["pods"]`

**`preconditions`** → **`spec.matchConditions`** (CEL):
- The old JMESPath expression `"org.com/role" AnyNotIn request.object.spec.tolerations[].key` becomes the CEL guard: `!has(object.spec.tolerations) || !object.spec.tolerations.exists(t, t.key == 'org.com/role')`

**`mutate.patchesJson6902`** → **`spec.mutations[].patchType: JSONPatch`** (CEL):
- The inline YAML patch is expressed as a CEL `JSONPatch` list appending the toleration object to `/spec/tolerations/-`

apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: block-stale-images
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Block Stale Images
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Images that are old usually have some open security vulnerabilities which are not patched.
      This policy checks the contents of every container image and inspects them for the create time.
      If it finds any image which was built more than 6 months ago this policy blocks the deployment.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit 
  rules:
    - name: block-stale-images
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Images built more than 6 months ago are prohibited."
        foreach:
        - list: "request.object.spec.containers"
          context:
          - name: imageData
            imageRegistry:
              reference: "{{ element.image }}"
          deny:
            conditions:
              all:
                - key: "{{ time_since('', '{{ imageData.configData.created }}', '') }}"
                  operator: GreaterThan
                  value: 4380h
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: block-stale-images
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Block Stale Images
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Images that are old usually have some open security vulnerabilities which are not patched.
      This policy checks the contents of every container image and inspects them for the create time.
      If it finds any image which was built more than 6 months ago this policy blocks the deployment.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ''
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  variables:
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers
        + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([])
        + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(container,
          image.GetMetadata(container.image).config.created == "" ||
          timestamp(image.GetMetadata(container.image).config.created) >
          timestamp(string(now() - duration("4380h")))
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        'Images built more than 6 months ago are prohibited. Stale images: ' +
        variables.allContainers.filter(container,
          image.GetMetadata(container.image).config.created != "" &&
          timestamp(image.GetMetadata(container.image).config.created) <=
          timestamp(string(now() - duration("4380h")))
        ).map(container, container.image).join(', ')
